entries.description: Twitter/X workforce reduction: Approximately 6,000 employees laid off following $44 billion acquisition, reducing workforce from ~7,500 to ~1,500. Layoffs conducted with minimal notice, many without legally required WARN Act notification. Workers and their households affected. Household multiplier 2.53 applied per US Census Bureau 2024 (6,000 x 2.53 = 15,180).
entries.causation_evidence: Bloomberg reporting; internal Twitter communications; SEC filings; WARN Act violation lawsuits filed November 2022

entries.description: Tesla workforce reduction: Approximately 19,027 employees laid off, reducing global workforce from 140,473 to approximately 121,000. Largest single-year layoff in Tesla history. Household multiplier 2.53 applied per US Census Bureau 2024 (19,027 x 2.53 = 48,138).
entries.causation_evidence: Tesla SEC filing Q1 2024; Bloomberg workforce analysis; Reuters

entries.description: DOGE federal workforce disruption: Brookings Institution recorded 26,511 occasions where federal workers were fired then rehired due to errors and lack of planning. Workers experienced job loss, income disruption, and household instability before reinstatement. Household multiplier 2.53 applied per US Census Bureau 2024 (26,511 x 2.53 = 67,073).
entries.causation_evidence: Brookings Institution analysis March 2025; Irish Examiner reporting; federal court records

entries.description: NEH grant cancellations: 1,477 previously approved grants terminated using a flawed ChatGPT prompt — two DOGE employees fed grant summaries to ChatGPT asking if they relate to DEI — without scholarly review or individualized assessment. Grants included Indigenous language preservation, Holocaust documentary, Black newspaper digitization, Appalachian photograph archives, and Italian-American history. Organizations in all 50 states affected. Conservative estimate: 44 community beneficiaries per grant (1,477 x 44 = 65,000 approx). DOGE staff communicated via Signal set to auto-delete, destroying the decision record in violation of the Federal Records Act — constituting active concealment of process.
entries.causation_evidence: ACLS/AHA/MLA lawsuit filing March 2026; court depositions of Justin Fox and Nathan Cavanaugh; Inside Higher Ed; O Globo; New York Times; Federal Records Act violation documented in court discovery

entries.description: NEH staff terminations: Approximately 65% of NEH full-time staff terminated as part of DOGE restructuring. NEH had approximately 150 full-time staff prior to cuts, resulting in approximately 98 workers losing employment. Household multiplier 2.53 applied per US Census Bureau 2024 (98 x 2.53 = 248).
entries.causation_evidence: Inside Higher Ed reporting; American Historical Association reporting; NEH staff records

entries.description: SEC settlement over 'funding secured' tweet: Musk posted about taking Tesla private at $420/share with 'funding secured' without verified funding. SEC charged securities fraud. Settlement required Musk to step down as Tesla chairman for 3 years and pay $20 million fine. Individual investor losses documented but number of distinct harmed individuals not verifiable from public record without further discovery. Entry retained at zero pending investor class identification.
entries.causation_evidence: SEC settlement records; court filings September 2018

entries.description: SEC lawsuit over late Twitter stock disclosure: Musk disclosed 5% Twitter stake 11 days past legal deadline in March 2022, allegedly allowing him to underpay by at least $150 million. SEC alleges investors sold shares at artificially depressed prices during the delay period. Parties in settlement talks as of March 2026. Number of distinct harmed investors not verifiable from public record without further discovery.
entries.causation_evidence: SEC court filing; Anadolu Ajansı; Chosunbiz reporting March 2026

entries.description: Musk publicly compared Grok's lack of content guardrails to VHS winning the format war by allowing pornographic content: 'VHS won in the end, in part because they allowed spicy mode.' Statement made with full knowledge of Grok's image generation capabilities. Subsequently cited in multiple lawsuits as documented evidence of deliberate design choice to enable CSAM and non-consensual intimate imagery generation. Harm count reflects 23,338 child victims documented by Center for Countering Digital Hate in the December 2025-January 2026 measurement period — the direct population harmed by the design choice this statement represents.
entries.causation_evidence: X post August 11 2025; USA Today; Center for Countering Digital Hate report January 2026; cited in Doe 1 v. X.AI Corp. (Case No. 5:26-cv-02246) and Ashley St. Clair lawsuit as evidence of deliberate design intent

entries.description: Tesla electric vehicle adoption: Over 4.2 million Tesla EVs sold globally as of 2024. Each vehicle represents a household transition away from internal combustion engine emissions. Tesla customers collectively avoided releasing 32 million metric tons of CO2e in 2024 per Tesla Impact Report — though independent analysis suggests figures may be overstated by up to 49%. Entry uses conservative vehicle owner count (4.2 million) rather than contested emissions claims. Confidence MEDIUM: vehicle sales verified; environmental benefit magnitude partially contested.
entries.causation_evidence: Tesla 2024 Impact Report; Sustainability Magazine July 2025; Carbon Credits independent study March 2025; IEA global EV market data 2024

entries.description: SpaceX reusable rocket program 2024: SpaceX led global orbital launches with 138 missions in 2024 — nearly half of all global launches. Reusable Falcon 9 reduced cost per kilogram to orbit from $10,000 to approximately $2,500, enabling missions previously impossible for smaller operators. NASA saved over $500 million on Crew Dragon launches using reusable boosters. Entry uses 138 verified launch missions as num_affected — each a mission enabled or made dramatically cheaper by reusability. Downstream beneficiaries include satellite internet users, climate monitoring, and scientific research globally.
entries.causation_evidence: ABI Research SpaceX analysis 2024; SpaceNews cost analysis; NASA Crew Dragon savings documentation; FAA 2024 launch records

entries.description: Tesla clean energy employment: Tesla has created more than 125,000 clean energy jobs globally as of 2024, spanning EV manufacturing, solar installation, battery production, and energy storage. These represent documented employment surplus in the transition away from fossil fuel industries — jobs that did not exist in this form before Tesla's market creation.
entries.causation_evidence: Tesla 2024 Extended Impact Report; Sustainability Magazine October 2025

entries.description: First successful orbital class rocket booster landing: Falcon 9 first stage landed successfully December 21 2015 — first time in history an orbital class rocket booster was recovered intact for reuse. This milestone enabled the entire reusable rocket economy. Population benefit tracked through musk_surplus_002 to avoid double-counting. Entry retained as historical record of the foundational technological event.
entries.causation_evidence: SpaceX launch records; NASA documentation; FAA licensing records December 2015
